'''Kasarax''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of the sauropteroid [[Swimming People of Dellalt|Swimming People]] of [[Dellalt/Legends|Dellalt]], who served as a fearsome example of his [[Species/Legends|species]] with fangs longer than a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Limb/Legends|forearm]]. By [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], the hot-headed and wild Kasarax was known as the "Top Bull" of a pack of Swimmers on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], and ran an operation offering rides across his [[Water/Legends|lake]] to land-dwelling [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] on his back for a hefty fee: 40 [[Drift (currency)|drift]] for each passenger.<ref name="hll" />

Kasarax's racket employed a large shore gang that kept competitors out of the market. Kasarax had wrested the title of Top Bull away from his uncle [[Shazeen]], who became a strenuous opponent of Kasarax's dominance. That rivalry came to a head with the arrival of [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and his crew at Kasarax's lake: when Solo chose to cross the lake on Shazeen's back, it led to a battle between the two Swimmers' factions. Kasarax attempted to challenge Shazeen directly during the fighting, only for the weathered old Swimmer to outsmart and defeat Kasarax, forcing the Top Bull to submit. The defeat led to the downfall of Kasarax's racket, and the dissolution of his shore gang.<ref name="hll" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kasarax first appeared in ''[[Han Solo and the Lost Legacy]]'', a novel written by [[Brian Daley]] and released in [[1980]].<ref name="hll" />
